observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting
Municipal General Services Officer to expedite the preparation of all necessary
documents, proceed with the appraisal and prioritize the immediate disposal of the
unserviceable properties to prevent further deterioration of the same and to realize
additional income from the proceeds of saleable items. Afterwards, provide the
Municipal Accountant with the copy of completed IIRUP to support the dropping of
disposed PPEs in the books of accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting
Municipal General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to complete the
reconciliation of the affected PPE accounts pursuant to Section 114 of MNGAS for
LGUs, Volume I. Henceforth, locate the missing documents and make the necessary
adjustments in their records, if warranted.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting MGSO to:
(a) expedite the preparation of all the necessary documents, proceed with the appraisal and prioritize the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties to avoid its further deterioration and possible loss, and additional income that could be derived therefrom; and
(b) complete the reconciliation of PPE accounts with the Accounting Office for the fair presentation of PPE accounts in the financial statements pursuant to Paragraph 114 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction:
Series of discussions on the disposal of unserviceable properties was made by the Municipal
Appraisal and Disposal Reiterated in Audit Observation 2021. No. 1, Page Nos. 33 and 34, Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: The appraisal and disposal of unserviceable properties was not made during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting MGSO to expedite the updating of the stock cards and to coordinate with the Municipal Accounting to ensure these are always reconciled with the ledger cards of the accounting office in accordance with Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, thereby ensuring that the actual physical count of inventory in the RPCI with reference to the stock cards are also reconciled and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted in order to establish the correct balances for the fair presentation of the Inventory accounts in the financial statements pursuant to Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Management already complied with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the:
• Sangguniang Bayan to pass a Resolution authorizing her to file the request for write-off of dormant receivables; and
• Municipal Accountant to facilitate the gathering and documents enumerated under COA Circular No. 2016-005 and attach the same for the filing of the request for write-off to the COA Audit Team;
• In the meantime, set up the provision for Allowance for Impairment in order to present fairly the Receivables account in the financial statement at net realizable value consistent with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction:
The Sanggunian passed Resolution No. 049-2021 on April 19, 2021 authorizing the Municipal Mayor to file the request for write-off of dormant receivables.
The Municipal Accountant facilitated the gathering and gathered the documents and filed the request for write-off to the Audit Team on June 9, 2021.
The Municipal Accountant set-up Allowance for Impairment of Receivables under Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 100-2021-03-00773 amounting to P251,643.38 dated March 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to effect the cancellation of the stale checks and request the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the Municipality’s books of accounts following the guidelines under Sections 59 and 61 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to give notice to the payees or holders of the checks at least one (1) month before the checks become stale.
observations.managementAction: The Management already complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal General Services Officer to:
• Ensure that the property insurance for the building totaling P16.377 million will be secured once constructions/improvements are completed; and
• Henceforth, ensure that all insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate property insurance with GSIS pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 565.
observations.managementAction: Waiting for the completion of the ongoing constructions/ improvements of the DA Building, Annex Building, and Polillo Public Market. Insurable properties other than those under constructions/ improvements are already secured with appropriate property insurance with GSIS.
observations.reason: The project constructions/improvements not yet applicable due to ongoing construction/improvements of the DA Building, Annex Building, and Polillo Public Market. The Audit Team shall monitor compliance of the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Agriculturist to continuously coordinate with the DA-RFO IV-A on the status of their request for technical assistance in order to facilitate the preparation of updated project profile and amendment of the Memorandum of Agreement with the DA-RFO IV-A for its approval. Thereafter, proceed with the implementation of the irrigation project.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculturist thru letter dated November 19, 2021, made final request for change of location of the project from the DA RFO IV-A with no objection from the latter thru letter dated December 15, 2021. Thereafter, proceed with the implementation of the irrigation project. The project documents are already in the Municipal Engineer’s Office as of December 31, 2021, and the implementation of the irrigation project commenced in the 1st quarter of 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all the concerned persons/departments to submit their actions plans and status of implementation to his Office and consolidate the same using the printed form or by way of electronic document for submission to the Audit Team within 60 days from receipt of the AAR.
observations.managementAction: The Management already complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Point System Technical Working Group to submit the Gender and Development Plan and Budget and its corresponding Accomplishment Report within the prescribed period as required under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and COA Circular 2014-001.
observations.managementAction: The Management already complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• instruct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resource Officer to comply with the recommendations of the DENR-EMB for revision of the Municipality’s 10-year SWMP to ensure its approval; and
• secure authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to enter into a Lease Agreement with the landowner on the use of the land as sanitary landfill site of the Municipality and thereafter, immediately direct the closure of the controlled dumpsite.
observations.managementAction:
Revised copy of the Municipality’s 10-year SWMP (Annex G) was submitted to the DENR-EMB on May 21, 2021.
The Sanggunian passed Resolution No. 057-2021 on May 10, 2021 authorizing the Municipal Mayor to enter into Contract of Lease with the landowner. Thereafter, Lease Contract with the landowner was secured on May 11, 2021 and the controlled dumpsite was closed in 2021.
observations.reason: The 10-year SWMP was not yet approved. Results of evaluation of the Municipality’s submitted SWMP made by the DENR-EMB showed some inconsistencies that need be addressed for the approval of the plan.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting MGSO to (a) expedite the preparation of all the necessary documents, proceed with the appraisal and prioritize the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties to avoid its further deterioration and possible loss, and additional income that could be derived therefrom; and (b) complete the reconciliation of PPE accounts with Accounting Office for the fair presentation of PPE accounts in the financial statements pursuant to Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting MGSO to expedite the updating of the stock cards and to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to ensure these are always reconciled with the ledger cards of the accounting office in accordance with Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, thereby ensuring that the actual physical count of inventory in the RPCI with reference to the stock cards are also reconciled with the accounting records. The Municipal Accountant and the Acting MGSO should facilitate the reconciliation of their records and investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted in order to establish the correct balances for the fair presentation of the Inventory accounts in the financial statements pursuant to Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the:
• Sangguniang Bayan to pass a Resolution authorizing her to file the request for authority for write-off of dormant receivables;
• Municipal Accountant to facilitate the gathering and completion of documents enumerated under COA Circular No. 2016-005 and attach the same for the filing of the request for write-off to the COA Audit Team; and
• in the meantime, set up the provision for Allowance for Impairment in order to present fairly the Receivables account in the financial statements at net realizable value consistent with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 and as required under Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to effect the cancellation of the stale checks and request the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the Municipality’s books of accounts following the guidelines under Sections 59 and 61 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to give notice to the payees or holders of checks at least one (1) month before the checks become stale.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal General Services Officer to:
• ensure that the property insurance for the buildings totaling P16,377,286.32 will be secured once construction/improvements are completed; and
• henceforth, ensure that all insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate property insurance with GSIS pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 565.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Agriculturist to continuously coordinate with the DA-RFO IV-A on the status of their request for technical assistance in order to facilitate the preparation of updated project profile and cause the amendment of the Memorandum of Agreement with the DA RFO-4A for its approval. Thereafter, proceed with the implementation of the irrigation project.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all the concerned persons/ departments to submit their action plans and status of implementation to his Office and consolidate the same using the AAPSI form, either in printed form or by way of electronic document for submission to the Audit Team within 60 days from receipt of the AAR.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to prepare all the necessary documents, ensure completing the appraisal and its immediate disposal in order to avoid further deterioration and possible loss, and generate additional income therefrom. Thereafter, require the completion of the reconciliation of the PPE accounts, investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted in order to establish its correct balances.
observations.managementAction: The Acting MGSO established a system/ database to keep track of all PPE and is still in the process of locating necessary documents for the appraisal of the unserviceable properties.
observations.reason: The appraisal of unserviceable properties was put on hold in CY 2020 because they prioritized activities related to COVID-19.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of supplies/inventories initially as an asset account and recognize the expense account once the inventories are consumed/distributed for proper matching of asset and expense accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already complied with the requirement of recording all purchases of supplies as inventory.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to update the SLCs for Inventory accounts. Thereafter, reconcile it with the records of the Accounting Office and the Acting Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) and investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted in order to establish its correct balances.
observations.managementAction: The SLCs were maintained and updated by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Stock cards were not updated as of year-end due to retirement of the assigned personnel. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under audit observation no. 2, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • MT to pursue the collection of the P104,467.40 by sending demand letters to the identified debtors; • Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate the supporting documents to identify the debtors and cause the Municipal Agriculturist to prepare schedule for the amount of P119,231.72 to enforce collection thereof, and if found to be futile, refer to Commission on Audit (COA) Circular 2016-005 for the request for write-off of dormant accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer, with the help of the Municipal Agriculturist sent demand letters on July 29 and December 23, 2020 to identified debtors with known address. Also, the Municipal Accountant submitted the list of debtors per available records to the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist to properly account the beneficiaries.
observations.reason: No collections/payments were received. Unknown debtors could no longer be traced. With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under audit observation no. 3, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate property insurance with the GSIS and to expedite the gathering of all supporting documents required by the GSIS for the immediate processing of their application for property insurance and providing the necessary funding thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Acting MGSO gave the assurance to fully implement the recommendations.
observations.reason: Ongoing

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Agriculturist to inquire on the status of their request for change of project location; and/or require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to return the total amount of P36,975.85 to the Source Agency.
observations.managementAction:
The request for change of location was approved by the Regional Director of DA-RFO 4A on October 28, 2019.
Refunded to DA the amount of P32,598.00 under Official Receipt No. 8506069 dated June 19, 2020, and remitted to Bureau of Treasury the 5% share of DPWH amounting P4,377.85 under Check No. 660103 dated June 8, 2020.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • Municipal Human Resource Management (MHRM) Assistant to ensure the completeness of all employees’ DTRs and CS Form 6 before forwarding to the Accounting Office for processing; and • Municipal Accountant to process only payrolls with complete supporting documents to establish validity and legality of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: The MHRM Assistant ensured the completeness of all employees’ DTRs and CS Form 6 before forwarding to the Accounting Office for processing. Municipal Accountant processed only payrolls with complete supporting documents.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: • Project Management and Implementing Unit (MPMIU) Head to submit to the Audit Team the approved suspensions for the period March 2019 to December 2019 supported with Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA) weather report, the Catch-up Plans and revised construction schedules for the evaluation of the Audit Team as well as the minutes of meeting on January 27, 2020 or any equivalent document to support the claim that an extension was approved by the Department of Agriculture (DA), Regional Field Office (RFO) No. 4A – PRDP; and for the completion of the PRDP subproject; and • coordinate with the contractor to address the cause/s of delays, and strictly monitor the progress of the contractor in order to fast track the accomplishment within the new target date.
observations.managementAction: Regular meetings and coordination with the contractor and Philippine Rural Development Project (PRDP) representatives were facilitated by the Municipal Mayor and MPMIU Head which resulted to the issuances of warnings and notices of termination due to delays and poor performance of the contractor where slippage exceeds 15% slippage.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: • instruct the Municipal Environment and Natural Resource Officer to comply with the recommendations of the DENR-EMB for the revision of the Municipality’s 10-year SWMP to ensure its approval; and • secure authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to enter into a Lease Agreement with the land owner on the use of the land as sanitary landfill site of the Municipality and thereafter, immediately direct the closure of the controlled dumpsite.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO commented that revisions had been made on the recommendations of DENR-EMB evaluators for its approval. The Municipal Mayor had secured authority to enter into a Lease Agreement with the land owner on the use of the land as sanitary landfill site under Sangguniang Bayan (SB) Resolution No. 057-2021 dated May 10, 2021.
observations.reason: The 10-year SWMP was returned to the Municipality for further revisions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting MGSO to prepare all
the necessary documents, ensure to complete the appraisal of all unserviceable
properties and cause its immediate disposal in order to avoid further deterioration
and possible loss, and to generate additional income from its disposal. Thereafter,
require the completion of the reconciliation of the PPE accounts investigate and
adjust any discrepancy that will be noted in order to establish its correct balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
record the purchases of supplies/inventories initially as an asset account and
recognize the expense account once the inventories are consumed/ distributed for
proper matching of asset and expense accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
update the SLCs for Inventory accounts. Thereafter, reconcile it with the records
of the Office of the GSO and investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be
noted in order to establish its correct balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• MT to pursue the collection of P104,467.40 by sending demand letters to the
identified debtors; and
• Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to locate the supporting documents
to identify the debtors and cause the preparation of schedule for the amount of
P119,231.72 to enforce collection thereof, and if found to be futile, refer to
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular 2016-005 for the request for write-off of
dormant accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting MGSO to ensure that
all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate
insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-
390 and RA No. 656 by expediting the gathering of all the supporting documents
required by the GSIS for the immediate processing of their application for property
insurance and providing the necessary funding thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Agriculturist
to inquire on the status of their request for change of project location and reiterate
our recommendation to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to return
the total amount of P36,975.85 to Source Agency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• Municipal Human Resource Management Assistant (MHRMA) to ensure the
completeness of all employees’ DTRs and CS Form 6 before forwarding to the
Accounting Office for processing; and
• Municipal Accountant to process only payrolls with complete supporting
documents to establish validity and legality of the transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Acting General Services Officer (GSO) to expedite the completion of the reconciliation of the PPE accounts in order to establish the correct balances, and to investigate and adjust any discrepancies that will be noted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Acting GSO is still in the process of reconciliation. With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented under Observation No. 1, Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: Due to lack of personnel to finish the physical inventory of the properties of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting GSO to expedite gathering of necessary information for the immediate preparation of IIRUP to enable the disposal committee to undertake immediate disposal of unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration and possible loss and to generate additional income from its disposal.
observations.managementAction: The Acting GSO prepared the IIRUP with incomplete data as of December 31, 2019 and provided the Audit Team with a copy.
observations.reason:
The Appraisal Committee are still in the process of conducting the appraisal of all the unserviceable properties of the Municipality.
With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented under Observation No. 1, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• Acting GSO to conduct the physical count of all inventories of the Municipality; prepare and submit immediately the inventory report (RPCI) thereon; and maintain the stock cards for the inventories; and
• Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC) for inventories; and
• Acting GSO and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records regularly; and
• Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary entries to adjust the affected accounts, if warranted.
observations.managementAction:
Conducted the physical count of inventories and prepared the RPCI and the stock cards. The Municipal Accountant is in the process of updating supplies ledger cards for CY 2020 and assured that she will regularly update and reconcile her records with that of the Office of the MGSO to establish the correct inventory account balances.
Not Implemented. Not Implemented. (for other items)
observations.reason:
Due to the continued practice of recording the supplies as outright expense which do not need ledger cards.
With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented under Observation Nos. 2 and 3, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting GSO to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656 by expediting the gathering of all the supporting documents required by the GSIS for the immediate processing of their application for property insurance and providing the necessary funding thereof.
observations.managementAction: The application for insurance of the Fishermen’s Multi-Purpose Trading, Evacuation Center and Community Fish Landing Center is still pending at the GSIS for inspection and appraisal of the building. In addition, the Municipal Mayor through, the offices concerned are gathering the necessary supporting documents for the immediate processing of the insurance of all insurable properties of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Three buildings with total cost of P11,008,080.91 were already insured. With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented under Observation No. 5, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC- Municipal Agriculturist to: follow up the availability of the accredited trainer and schedule the training for the operation of the Mini Feed Mill; coordinate with the Department of Agriculture for the provision of additional budget and release of funds for the project implementation; and coordinate with the Bids and Awards Committee for the immediate procurement of 4-wheel drive truck and in case the second bidding fails, negotiated procurement may be resorted to as provided for in Section 53.1 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184, otherwise known as the Government Procurement Reform Act.
observations.managementAction: Request letter was submitted to the office of the Bottom-Up Planning and Budgeting (BUB) Focal Person and promised them that they will send their personnel to assist in the preparation of detailed design and program of works. The OIC- Municipal Agriculturist conducted the training on June 1 to 5, 2019. Procured the four-wheel drive truck. Which was delivered on December 14, 2019.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented. The request for change of project location by DA-RFO4A is pending approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to release the RPT share of the component barangays promptly within five days after the end of each quarter in accordance with Section 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The RPT share for CY 2019 were distributed to the Barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the heads of Offices involved in the processing and approval of loan application to coordinate to ensure that an employee’s net take home pay shall not be less than the mandated minimum allowed take home pay of P5,000.00 for CY 2018 as provided under Section 48 of the GAA for FY 2018. Henceforth, see to it that the mandatory monthly net take home pay provided in the GAA is strictly observed yearly.
observations.managementAction: No more loan was approved for employees during the year for the 21 employees. Strictly follows the mandatory net take home pay provided in the GAA. The Municipal Mayor ensures the mandatory net take home pay is strictly observed yearly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting MPDC to expedite the completion of CBMS that will establish/create a GAD Database that will assist the gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Database was already established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor utilize the 20% Development Fund through proper planning on the PPAs to be procured and/or implemented during the year and to coordinate with the winning bidder to cause the immediate submission of CSHP so that all the programmed development projects could be implemented immediately and completed within the year to ensure that intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The 20% Development Fund for CY 2019 were utilized accordingly. The contractors were immediately required to submit their approved CSHP or even the proof of application.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the new Municipal Disaster Risk Management Officer (MDRRMO) to request for additional budget/funds to maximize the utilization of LDRRM Fund through proper planning so that all its planned PPAs for disaster risk preparedness are fully implemented on the targeted implementation period to ensure the Municipality’s preparedness to natural and man-made hazard/disasters.
observations.managementAction: Utilization of 70% LDRRM Fund allocated for disaster preparedness were maximized. Moreover, the acquisition of truck elf was also funded and procured during the year 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MENRO to update its 10-year SWMP from 2017-2026 to 2018-2027 and the WACS as required by EMB; and to coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan for the authority to purchase a lot for the development and operation of a sanitary landfill as final disposal site pursuant to the provisions of the existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO already submitted the 10-year SWMP to the Regional Office of DENR-EMB on February 28, 2020 together with the safe closure and rehabilitation plan. The proposed landfill was already surveyed and the plan was prepared for the final negotiation with the land owner.
observations.reason: The submitted WACS has errors and needed correction. The plan was corrected only in the last quarter of 2019 with the help of DENR-EMB. With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented under Observation No. 9, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor initiate the CBMS and establish a GAD Database that will assist the gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented anew under Observation No. 11, Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: Due to non-completion of the CBMS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and
the Acting General Services Officer to expedite the completion of the
reconciliation of the property, plant and equipment account in order to establish
the correct PPE balances, and to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will
be noted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting GSO to expedite
gathering of necessary information for the immediate preparation of IIRUP to
enable the disposal committee to undertake immediate disposal of unserviceable
properties to avoid further deterioration and possible loss and to generate
additional income from its disposal and for the fair presentation of the financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting GSO to ensure that
all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate
insurance with the GSIS in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-
390 and RA No. 656.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the Acting GSO to provide the necessary supporting documents
required by the GSIS to immediately process their application for property
insurance of all the insurable properties of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Agriculturist
to:
  - follow up the availability of the accredited trainer and schedule the training for the
operation of Mini Feed Mill;
  - coordinate with the Department of Agriculture for the provision of additional budget
and release of funds for the project implementation; and
  - coordinate with the Bids and Awards Committee for the immediate procurement of 4-
wheel drive truck and in case the second bidding fails, negotiated procurement may
be resorted to as provided for in Section 53.1 of the 2016 Revised Implementing
Rules and Regulations of RA 9184, otherwise known as the Government
Procurement Reform Act.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
release the RPT share of the component Barangays promptly within five days after
the end of each quarter in accordance with Section 36 of COA Circular No. 92-382
and Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the heads of Offices involved
in the processing and approval of loan application to coordinate to ensure that an
employees net take home pay shall not be less than the mandated minimum
allowed take home pay of P5,000.00 for CY 2018 as provided under Section 48 of
the GAA for FY 2018.

Henceforth, see to it that the mandatory monthly net take home pay provided in the
GAA is strictly observed yearly.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Acting General Services Officer to reconcile the property, plant and equipment account in order to establish the correct PPE balances, and to investigate and adjust any discrepancy that will be noted.
observations.managementAction: On-going Implemented With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented under Observation No. 1, Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:  require the Acting GSO to prepare the IIRUP duly supported with complete documentation; and  create a Committee to undertake immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties, to avoid further deterioration and possible loss and to generate additional income from its disposal.
observations.managementAction: On-going Implemented Disposal committee was already created thru Executive Order No. 28-2018 on December 14, 2018
observations.reason: Lack of information on unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to integrate the Construction Safety and Health Program in the computation of construction costs of all projects to be implemented by the Municipality and require the contractors to submit Construction Safety and Health Program duly-approved by DOLE upon signing of the contracts.
observations.managementAction: Implemented The Contractors are being required to submit their CSHP duly-approved by DOLE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Waste Management Board, secure the approval of its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan from the National Solid Waste Management Commission and effect the provisions in their framework and develop and operate a sanitary landfill site as final disposal site pursuant to the provisions of the existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Audit Observation is updated and presented under Observation No. 4, Part II of this Report. The acquisition of the land ideal for the development of sanitary landfill is already endorsed to the Sangguniang Bayan for their approval.
observations.reason: Lack of land.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MPDC to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through proper planning so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented immediately and completed within the year to ensure that intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Implemented Partially Implemented With the new developments, the Audit Observation is updated and presented under Observation No. 5, Part II of this Report.
observations.reason: Change of supervision in the BAC; delayed submission of CSHP and delayed preparation of program of works.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete set of subsidiary ledgers for all economic enterprises operated by the Municipality and prepare the required Financial Statements and supporting schedules in compliance with Section 313 of RA No. 7160 and Sections 105 and 110 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The complete sets of subsidiary ledgers, financial statements and supporting schedules were already prepared by the Municipality and Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to review the cashbook entries and compare her cashbook entries with the accounting records and effect adjustments for the reconciling items, if any, to ensure reconciliation and reliability of the cashbook balances.
observations.managementAction: The cashbook entries are now reconciled with the accounting records and effect adjustments for the reconciling items were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct all concerned Department Heads involved in the processing of payments to ensure that near maturing obligations are handled with utmost care to avoid unnecessary penalties; and to refund the amount of penalties for late payments.
observations.managementAction: Refunded the amount of P2,947.51 for the penalties for late payments. Payment of Official Receipt No. 639646 dated May 17, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Disaster Risk Management Officer (MDRRMO) and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to ensure that planned PPAs for disaster risk management are fully implemented on target implementation period to ensure the Municipality's preparedness to natural and man-made hazards/disasters.
observations.managementAction: Some prior year’s PPAs for DRRM were already implemented.
observations.reason: Change of supervision in the DRRMO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the In-Charge of Office
(OIC), General Services Office (GSO) to submit the RPCPPE, update the property
cards and coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of the PPEL
balance with the PPELC. Observe the provisions of Section 124 of the MNGAS,
Volume I on the prescribed timeline in the submission of RPCPPE to allow the
necessary verification and reconciliation.

Also, we recommended to require the Accounting Office to update the PPELC to
provide sufficient basis for the correct provision of depreciation periodically.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to determine the mode of acquisition of parcels of land for purposes of determining
whether acquisition cost or fair value thereof will be used in recognizing them in the
books of accounts and take the appropriate action thereon.

As regards the donated two parcels of land worth £550,000.00 for which
negotiation is on-going for the return to the Municipality, such information should
be disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit liquidation report on the
cash advance for confidential expenses amounting to £15,000.00 to the Intelligence
and Confidential Fund Audit Unit (ICFAU), Office of the Chairman, Commission on
Audit as required under COA-DBM-DILG-GCG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-
001 dated January 8, 2015, and direct:

1. Ms. Christine G. Llacuna to settle immediately her outstanding cash advances
amounting to £127,620.00.

2. the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all cash advances are fully
liquidated at the end of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

> provide the necessary support in enjoining the remaining barangays to
establish their own MRF and to strictly implement waste segregation at
source;
> strictly enforce and intensify the information campaign on the no-littering
policy among the residents;

> develop and operate a sanitary landfill as a final disposal site for solid and,
eventually, residual wastes of the Municipality; and

> strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines under RA
No. 9003.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to confirm with account owners/creditors and to request them to submit documents
pertaining to their claims to the Municipality. In the event that they fail to support
their claims, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund,
subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan with a condition that
appropriation shall be made available in the event that in the future claim arises,
pursuant to the provision of Section 98 of PD No. 1445,

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Engineer to submit the list of all on-going government
projects/programs/activities (PPA) and those that are to be implemented during the
year. Likewise, submit the monthly monitoring report in compliance with the above-
cited regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
strictly comply with the requirements stated under COA Circular No. 92-389 to
prevent the possible re-use of those documents for other claims of same nature as
part of a sound internal control system in the disbursement of funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

e Bids and Awards Committee and the Acting General Service Officer to
procure commonly used supplies and equipment from the DBM-PS in
conformity with the above cited Administrative Order and DBM Circular
Letter No. 2013-14 to support the government’s policy for transparency in
government transactions and efficiency in procurement processes; and

e Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit the APP-CSE to DBM
pursuant to DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-16.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and
Development Council to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund
through proper planning so that all its programmed development projects could be
implemented immediately and completed within the year to ensure that intended
beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.

‘We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Health Officer submit to the Auditor
the request for exemption with justification duly approved by the NCPAM Program
Director, if any, for the drugs and medicines not included in the latest PNF Manual
as stated above, otherwise disallowed in audit,

Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-
Municipal Health Officer to procure only those drugs and medicines identified in the
PNF Manual and issue certification that products being requisitioned and procured
fall within and conform with the latest edition of the PNF Manual. Moreover, direct
the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all
purchases of drugs and medicines are supported with the said certification by the
requisitioning officer or by the duly authorized officer.
